mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and rising recession probability, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and business confidence, generating sustained earnings growth that pushes S&P 500 returns higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60 percent of cases over a rolling 2-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the index of industrial production reflects capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that trigger short-term profit margin compression and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO declines during periods identified as late-cycle expansions (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound eliminates further rate cut expectations and forces investors into equities for yield, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of trading days where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 55 percent of instances across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly translate into higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production drops below 95 percent of its 2017 baseline, manufacturing contraction signals imminent economic slowdown, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of observations where INDPRO falls below the 95 threshold.
